curation_notes: Re-curation with updated hints. The vulnerability is in src/utils/parse.js where the parse() function uses eval() to execute any string starting with 'function' as JavaScript code. The patch (merge commit c50bfff) replaced eval() with a regex-validated Function constructor and added an allowFunctionEvaluation prop. 4 non-test source files changed (JsonTree.js, JsonFunctionValue.js, JsonNode.js, parse.js), but only parse.js contains the actual vulnerability; the others wire through the new prop. Kept pre-assigned command-injection class — CWE-95 (Eval Injection) maps to dynamic code execution, and command-injection is the closest valid class for unsafe eval() usage among the 13 available classes.
